jobText:
React Developer
 віддалено
Tenexti is an emerging software development company based in Lviv. We specialize in creating innovative solutions, delivering high-quality results, and helping our clients turn their ideas into reality. With a passion for technology and a commitment to excellence, we strive to build lasting partnerships and drive meaningful impact through our work.

We are looking for a Frontend engineer to join our team.

🧑‍💻 What we expect from the ideal candidate:
You have 2–3 years of experience working with React in production environments.
You are a good engineer. You don’t write code just to the point where it starts working. You go the extra mile to make your code elegant, simple, robust, and easy to maintain.
You have strong TypeScript and React proficiency. You understand component architecture, state management, API integration, performance, and frontend best practices.
You have good UX taste. You care not only about how the code works, but also about how the product feels. You notice rough edges, think about users, and want to build interfaces that are clear, fast, and pleasant to use.
You think beyond just coding. Building great software isn’t about closing tickets — it’s about making the product and codebase better every time you touch it. You ask questions, care about the “why”, and think about the real business impact of your work.
You are open-minded and eager to learn. You are interested in modern frontend tools, patterns, and approaches, and you are excited to apply them in real production systems.
You are a good team player. You treat people around you well and have strong communication skills to articulate technical decisions clearly.
You don’t need supervision. You are self-driven and can solve problems on your own. You take initiative when you see opportunities for improvement.
💡 What you will do:
Work on two main products of the company. One product is already built with React, while the second one currently has a legacy Vue.js frontend that needs to be rewritten using a modern React stack.
Maintain and improve the existing React product. You will add new features, build new pages, improve existing flows, and continuously make the user experience better.
Rewrite a legacy frontend from Vue.js to React. This is a great opportunity to build a modern frontend from the ground up using current best practices, clean architecture, and a thoughtful developer experience.
Create high-quality user interfaces. You will work not only on functionality, but also on usability, clarity, performance, and the overall feeling of the product.
Improve frontend architecture. You will help shape component structure, data-fetching patterns, reusable UI abstractions, routing, forms, error handling, and overall maintainability.
Collaborate with backend engineers. Most of the heavy business logic lives on the backend, but the frontend must represent it clearly and reliably for users.
Perform code reviews. We treat code reviews seriously. You will review pull requests, suggest cleaner and simpler approaches, and also receive thoughtful reviews from your peers.
Optionally explore backend Node.js services in the future. This is not required for the role, but if you are interested, there may be opportunities to look into our Node.js/NestJS ecosystem over time.
Team and Customer:

We are a team of engineers based in Lviv working for a US-based customer. All engineers and architectural decisions are here locally—no late calls with the US, no overseas knowledge holders blocking you.

Our customer is not backed by venture capital but by reliable business revenue. We’re self-funded and don’t experience pressure to cut roles in Ukraine due to "country risks"—quite common lately.

We’re hiring due to business growth. By leveraging technology and smart people, we empower the business to grow further.

Product:

We work on a high-stakes data synchronization system handling hundreds of thousands of records in real-time. The product ensures data consistency between multiple systems while operating under strict rate limits and performance constraints.

This is a fascinating technical challenge—not a simple CRUD application, but a complex, performance-sensitive system where every optimization matters, stability is non-negotiable, and creative problem-solving is daily routine.

Technical highlights:

Two major frontend products: one already built with React, and one legacy Vue.js frontend that will be rewritten using a modern React stack.
A browser extension: written in React that interacts with our system.
Large-scale production infrastructure: our systems run on a serious infrastructure footprint with around 1TB of RAM across the cluster and hundreds of CPU cores.
Complex distributed architecture: frontend applications interact with backend services that depend on multiple Node.js microservices, Java/Kotlin applications, RabbitMQ, Redis, MongoDB, PostgreSQL, Hasura, and GraphQL.
Business-critical workflows: the frontend is used to operate and understand complex synchronization flows, pricing logic.
High reliability expectations: mistakes can have serious business impact, so clarity, validation, observability, and thoughtful UX matter a lot.
🚀 Our technology stack:

Frontend:
React 19
TypeScript
Tailwindcss
Antd
Tanstack Query (react-query)
Tanstack Router
Vite
GraphQL
Vue.js legacy frontend — to be rewritten in React
Modern frontend tooling and best practices
Browser Extension:
React 18
TypeScript
Tanstack Query (react-query)
Tailwindcss
Antd
Backend:

Java 21 & Kotlin (actively migrating towards more Kotlin)
Spring Boot
MongoDb, Redis
GoRules for complex business logic
Infrastructure & DevOps:

Kubernetes
ArgoCD and Github Actions
OpenSearch for logs
Prometheus & Grafana for metrics & traces
The broader ecosystem:

TypeScript, Node.js & Nest.js microservices
PostgreSQL, Hasura & GraphQL
Why choose this project and team?

Stable project running successfully for 8+ years with consistent revenue growth
Entire R&D team in Ukraine making all architectural decisions locally
Mission-critical production system where your decisions directly impact business outcomes
Real engineering challenges: Performance optimization, rate limiting, data consistency, large-scale refactoring
Opportunity to work on a quite high load production system (Monolith + microservices) and have an impact on how it is designed and shaped.
Perks and Benefits:

🏋️‍♂️ Gym compensation

🏝18 days of paid vacation, public holidays, and paid sick leave

💻 Modern tech gear: MacBook based on silicon CPU (M1-M3) with 32 of RAM

🇬🇧 English lessons

⏱️ Flexible working hours and no time trackers

✈️ Possible trips to USA when the war ends. Before the war, we flew there twice and we loved it :)

If you read this far please start your message with “Glory to Ukraine!”, thank you!

must_have: 2–3 years of experience working with React in production environments
Self-driven, able to work without supervision
nice_to_have: Interest in or experience with Node.js/NestJS backend services
Knowledge of distributed systems or microservices architecture
responsibilities: Maintain and improve the existing React product
Add new features, build new pages, improve existing flows
Rewrite a legacy Vue.js frontend to React
Create high-quality user interfaces focused on usability, clarity, and performance
Improve frontend architecture: component structure, data-fetching patterns, reusable UI abstractions, routing, forms, error handling
Perform code reviews and suggest cleaner and simpler approaches
Optionally explore backend Node.js/NestJS services in the future
soft_signals: Ownership mentality — goes beyond closing tickets
Good UX taste — cares about how the product feels
Open-minded and eager to learn modern tools and patterns
Strong team player with clear communication of technical decisions
Asks questions and cares about the 'why'
Entire R&D team based locally in Ukraine — no late calls with US
Stable, self-funded (non-VC) company with 8+ years of revenue
Flexible working hours, no time trackers
red_flags: Seniority level is middle (2–3 years) but scope includes large-scale legacy rewrite and architecture decisions — may be demanding for the level
US-based customer with Ukraine-based team — potential for future timezone or communication friction
War-related context explicitly mentioned (travel to USA contingent on war ending)

tailored.headline: Frontend Engineer | React, TypeScript, TanStack Query, Vite
tailored.summary: Frontend Engineer with 5+ years of production experience building React and TypeScript applications end-to-end — from component architecture and data-fetching patterns to performance optimization and deployment. Comfortable owning full features independently: requirements, implementation, code review, and post-launch stability. Experienced collaborating with backend engineers, designers, and PMs in fast-moving SaaS and B2B product environments. Writes clean, reusable UI with a strong eye for usability and long-term maintainability.

bullets: Designed and built a monorepo architecture from scratch using Nx to unify 3 products (marketplace, admin panel, landing pages), maximizing reuse of UI components and business logic across the ecosystem

Owned the core React and TypeScript architecture of a P2P crypto marketplace (50K+ MAU), delivering secure authentication, a multi-step order creation engine, and advanced search with dynamic filtering
Built a real-time communication layer using WebSockets, enabling live chat messaging and transaction status synchronization between buyers and sellers
Delivered a full arbitration and management admin panel with financial dashboards, role-based access control (RBAC), subscription management, and dispute resolution tools
Developed performance-critical marketing landing pages with Next.js, integrating product analytics, tracking scripts, and A/B testing configurations that improved user conversion rates by ~15%
Performed regular code reviews across a team of 3–5 frontend developers, helping maintain consistent component architecture and engineering standards
Configured CI/CD pipelines, production monitoring, and logging to ensure reliable end-to-end delivery and post-launch stability
Collaborated directly with backend engineers, PMs, BAs, and founders to translate business requirements into technical tasks, estimates, and maintainable implementation plans
Owned the refactor and stabilization of an existing React admin dashboard — conducted a full code audit, fixed critical bugs in business logic, broken API requests, and improper error handling
Built a high-performance, SEO-optimized marketing landing page from scratch using Next.js, driving B2B user acquisition through optimized page speed and search rankings
Integrated GraphQL APIs with Apollo Client, implementing queries, mutations, caching, loading states, and consistent error handling across the dashboard
Implemented a secure subscription and billing system via Stripe integration, covering multi-tier pricing pages, billing management, and transaction status workflows
Developed an isolated embeddable chat widget using Shadow DOM for clean DOM containment and WebSockets for real-time AI response delivery on client websites
Built an Integrations Hub enabling users to connect third-party platforms (Slack, Telegram, Webhooks) to route leads and notifications from the chatbot
Optimized the data-fetching layer using TanStack Query, establishing robust caching, background revalidation, and consistent state synchronization across views
Improved client-side performance (LCP, TTI) through code-splitting and lazy loading, reducing overall bundle size by ~10% and widget load times by ~15%
Contributed to building and launching the platform from scratch to production, covering the admin panel, marketing modules, and real-time alert infrastructure
Built a high-performance, SEO-optimized marketing landing page using Next.js with semantic HTML, custom meta tags, and responsive layouts to maximize search ranking and conversion
Developed an interactive Widget Builder (Alert Box, Goal Progress) allowing streamers to customize colors, layouts, sounds, typography, and animation triggers for OBS overlays
Implemented OAuth integrations with Twitch and YouTube to streamline streamer authorization, profile synchronization, and live broadcast status tracking
Built data-heavy analytical dashboards with responsive data tables (1000+ rows), pagination, transaction filtering, and interactive financial charts for real-time revenue tracking
Designed multi-step configuration forms with advanced validation for donation thresholds, custom media file constraints, and profanity filtering
Integrated REST APIs and WebSockets for global state management using Redux and reliable real-time alert distribution, in close collaboration with backend engineers
Delivered pixel-perfect layouts from Figma designs using clean, maintainable SCSS, ensuring cross-browser compatibility across all device sizes

Pixel-perfect Figma delivery, multi-step forms, usability-focused dashboards, A/B testing

Listed in KYC Market tech stack

tailored.match_notes:
Kostya is a strong technical match for this role. His React, TypeScript, TanStack Query, GraphQL, Vite, and Tailwind CSS experience maps directly to the core stack. He has clear evidence of feature ownership, code reviews, performance optimization, and cross-team collaboration — all key signals the job is looking for.

The main gaps are: (1) No Vue.js experience, which matters because the role includes rewriting a legacy Vue frontend to React — Kostya can do the React side but has no documented Vue reading/migration experience; (2) No browser extension development; (3) No NestJS or backend service work beyond Node.js being listed as a skill; (4) Ant Design (Antd) is not in his stack — Material UI is the closest equivalent.

The seniority framing is the most sensitive point. Kostya's real experience is Senior-level (team lead, hiring, architecture ownership), but the role is formally scoped at Middle. The CV has been toned down to avoid overqualified signals, but interviewers may still probe why he is applying at this level. He should be ready to address this directly and frame it as a deliberate choice (product fit, team culture, stability of the company).

The ~15% conversion improvement metric and the bundle size figures should be backed up with real data if asked — interviewers at product companies often probe these.

format_checks.rule: All experience bullets start with an action verb
format_checks.note: DonateSystem bullet 1 starts with 'Contributed to building' — 'Contributed to' is a weak, passive-adjacent opener that dilutes ATS verb scoring. All other bullets pass.

format_checks.rule: No passive constructions (Responsible for / Helped with)
format_checks.note: DonateSystem bullet 1: 'Contributed to building and launching' is a passive-adjacent hedge. KYC Market bullet 6: 'helping maintain' inside the bullet is a gerund chain that weakens the action verb. These are not hard failures but are ATS yellow flags.

KYC Market bullet 1 ('Designed and built a monorepo architecture from scratch using Nx to unify 3 products (marketplace, admin panel, landing pages), maximizing reuse of UI components and business logic across the ecosystem') and KYC Market bullet 8 ('Collaborated directly with backend engineers, PMs, BAs, and founders to translate business requirements into technical tasks, estimates, and maintainable implementation plans') both risk wrapping to 3 lines in standard ATS renderers at 80-char width.

format_checks.rule: Standard sections present (headline, summary, skills, experience)
No special characters or table-like structures breaking text parsers
Job titles are ATS-standard (no creative/non-standard titles)
